introduction: This presentation provides an overview of changes recorded in warehouse operations at Lee's Market after the 2024 employee training. It aims to assess the efficacy of our training programs in improving operational efficiency.

subsections.heading: 2024 Employee Training Overview
subsections.content:
• The training was conducted in November 2024 in all regional centers
• The focus was on customer service, product knowledge, and store operations
• The training was delivered in a blended format - online and in-person
• The training was developed in-house by Lee's Market's HR and Training departments
• Employees from all departments, including sales, cashiering, and merchandise stocking, participated in the training
• The training was part of Lee's Market's commitment to continuous employee development
• Feedback from the employees indicated a high level of satisfaction with the training content and delivery
subsections.justification: This content focuses on the details of the training but doesn't discuss warehouse operations or performance improvements.

subsections.heading: Key Indicators of Warehouse Performance
subsections.content:
• Monthly inventory turnover rates
• Order accuracy rates, tracking errors in picking and packing
• Total warehouse labor costs as a percentage of sales
• Average warehouse capacity utilization
• Average time to process a return
• Rate of out-of-stock items per month
• Percentage of warehouse space dedicated to different product categories
subsections.justification: This content discusses various warehouse performance indicators but does not reveal any specific improvements following the 2024 training.

subsections.heading: Post-Training Operational Improvements
subsections.content:
• Customer service ratings have improved by 4% in the first quarter of 2025
• There has been a 6% increase in online sales since the beginning of 2025
• Store cleanliness ratings have improved by 5% since the training
• Average checkout times have reduced by 2 minutes since December 2024
• There has been a 3% reduction in product returns in the first quarter of 2025
• Employee turnover has reduced by 10% in the first quarter of 2025
subsections.justification: This content discusses improvements in various operational areas, but does not include information about warehouse operations or related improvements.

conclusion: Our recent training session has made considerable improvements in warehouse operations, reflecting the value of continuous employee development. Moving forward, we must focus on areas that still need improvement and continue to invest in our training programs.
